title: LMND Lemonade: When a 71% Revenue Surge Meets a Narrowing Loss
text: Lemonade delivered a 71% year-over-year revenue surge in Q1 2026, with earnings per share of -$0.29, beating consensus estimates of -$0.39 by approximately 25.6%. The company's annual revenue run-rate reached $615 million, with Q2 consensus estimates at $252 million. Despite the strong beat, shares experienced a modest decline post-announcement. The analysis attributes this to sector rotation dynamics rather than fundamental weakness. The market's indifference to a 71% revenue beat and a $0.10 EPS surprise is not a fundamental judgment — it's a positioning artifact. The narrowing loss trajectory alongside accelerating revenue suggests improving operating leverage. With TTM EPS at -$2.24 and quarterly compression visible, the company appears to be approaching an inflection point where fixed-cost absorption from technology infrastructure and claims automation becomes increasingly evident. The Q2 EPS estimate of -$0.57 likely reflects seasonal catastrophe loss assumptions rather than deterioration. The $252M revenue guidance appears conservative, with potential for upside if natural disaster activity runs below model expectations.
